subject: Sociology
title: Prestige and Status Symbols
description: One-minute skill: Prestige and Status Symbols.

hook.text: A doctor and a plumber earn similar salaries. But mention their jobs at a party and the reactions differ completely.

buildup.text: Prestige is the social respect attached to positions. It doesn't always match income but always shapes treatment.

discovery.text: Status symbols — designer clothes, luxury cars, degrees — signal position. People read them instantly.

twist.text: Veblen called it conspicuous consumption: spending to display status, not to meet needs. It persists everywhere.

climax.text: Prestige hierarchies vary by culture. A warrior's honor, a scholar's rank, a CEO's bonus — all serve the same function.

punchline.text: What you own tells the world where you stand.

quiz.question: What is conspicuous consumption?
options: Spending to display social status rather than meet needs
